title: Giveaway: Enter to win tickets to ‘The Drama’
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Enter by submitting a relationship question to Love Letters from March 24 at 10 a.m. to March 27 at 11 a.m.
Winners receive free tickets to an early screening of 'The Drama' in Somerville, starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson.
Entries are open to individuals aged 18 and older, no purchase necessary; winners notified by 5 p.m. on March 27.

title: Lowell was named the best place to live in Mass. But why?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Livability named Lowell the best place to live in Massachusetts due to its culture, community, and opportunities.
Arts, cuisine, and festivals highlight Lowell's strong community spirit, with the city hosting events like the Lowell Folk Festival.
Lowell emphasizes collaboration, seen in projects like the Hive Public Market, with education and affordable housing as key priorities.

title: Body of missing Holy Cross student recovered in Connecticut lake
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dominick Tocci, 20, was found after a canoe accident on Lake Hayward, East Haddam, Connecticut, during a multi-day search.
Tocci's body was recovered Monday afternoon by Connecticut Environmental Conservation Police after the canoe capsized on Saturday night.
Another student involved in the accident was rescued and discharged from the hospital; the cause of Tocci's death is under investigation.

title: Livestream: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague and is on trial for assault with a weapon.
Fitzsimmons has pleaded not guilty in the ongoing trial, with testimonies resuming via a livestream from NBC10 Boston.
The incident occurred last summer, and the trial proceedings are gaining public interest due to its unusual circumstances.

title: Where to find the best food under $20 in Malden
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sun Kong in Malden offers dim sum priced under $20, with three large plates costing just over $18.
Caliente Mexican Grill in Malden serves customizable birria tacos for $15, including three tacos, consomme, and rice.
Crying Thaiger on Ferry St. offers Thai dishes under $20, like the Crying Thaiger curry priced at $16.95 with freebies.

title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens leaves Boston College after two seasons, signing an AHL amateur tryout agreement with the Providence Bruins.
Hagens joins Providence Bruins' practice and is expected to debut against the Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday.
Drafted 7th overall in 2025, Hagens posted 47 points in 34 games during his sophomore year at Boston College.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer and Brayan Bello are key players for the Red Sox's 2026 success.
Outfielder Roman Anthony is hyped as a potential MVP candidate in his first full MLB season.
Prospect Payton Tolle could impact the Red Sox bullpen if injuries or performance issues arise.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones, Jr., State House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection, ending a 32-year tenure in office.
Jones, 61, has served as the representative for the 20th Middlesex District since March 1994 and as Minority Leader since 2002.
Massachusetts Republican Party Chair Amy Carnevale praised Jones for his dedication and impact over his legislative career.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: ICE agents have not been deployed to Logan Airport or any New England airports, as stated by TSA union president.
DHS officers conducted a specialized security operation at Logan Airport but did not include ICE agents.
ICE agents are deployed to 14 U.S. airports due to extended wait times amid an ongoing government shutdown.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, confirmed by Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is acting chief following Suarez's leave that began on February 26.
Details of the investigation remain undisclosed due to personnel matters, according to city officials.
